treatments.articleTitle: Insect Fauna associated with the Tea Ecosystem of North Bengal, India
treatments.articleAuthor: Mitra, Bulganin, Shah, Suresh Kumar & Mishra, Purnendu
treatments.journalTitle: Records of the Zoological Survey of India
treatments.chunk_text: Order COLEOPTERA Coleoptera (beetles and weevils) is the largest order in the class Insecta. They feed on a wide variety of diets, inhabit all terrestrial and fresh-water environments, and exhibit a number of diferent life styles. Many species are herbivores, adapted to feed on the roots, stems, leaves, or reproductive structures of their host plants. Some species live on fungi, others burrow into plant tissues and some excavate tunnels in wood or under bark. Many beetles are predators. Some beetles are scavengers, feeding primarily on carrion, decaying wood or other dead organic matter. 19 species under 16 genera belonging to 05 families of the order Coleoptera were reported from the tea gardens of North Bengal (Table 6). Of them, Scarabaeidae represent 09 Species followed by Cerambycidae (06 Species), Coccinellidae (02 Species), Lucanidae (01 species) and Curculionidae (01 species). treatments.treatmentTitle: Hydroscapha shuihau Fikáček & Hu & Aston & Jia & Liang & Liu & Minoshima 2020, new species
treatments.articleTitle: Comparative morphology of immature stages and adults of Hydroscapha from Taiwan, with description of a new species from Hong Kong (Coleoptera: Myxophaga: Hydroscaphidae)
treatments.articleAuthor: Fikáček, Martin, Hu, Fang-Shuo, Aston, Paul, Jia, Feng-Long, Liang, Wei-Ren, Liu, Hsing-Che & Minoshima, Yûsuke N.
treatments.chunk_text: Distribution. The species is only known from Lantau Island in Hong Kong. Bionomics (Fig. 8D, E). At the type locality, the beetles have been found in filamentous algae in a hygropetric environment on the concrete footpath leading from Shui Hau village to the sea. The footpath is edged by a freshwater marsh on both sides, which is mainly grassy and grazed by water buffalo. The beetles only occur in the hygropetric areas where the footpath has sunken and is covered by a very thin, almost stagnant film of water over a thin layer of mud and the filamentous algae. The beetles are usually present from November to April (mostly in the dry season) when the slope of the marsh allows the hygropetric environment to flourish. The algae die off before the hot summer months and again start to form mats in September; despite this, the beetles were not present until 9 November 2019, even though the filamentous mats of algae were fairly large (80 cm 2). We did not find any beetles in similar-looking habitats in areas

treatments.articleTitle: A revision of the genera Anilaroides Théry, 1934, stat. nov. and Tetragonoschema Thomson, 1857 (Coleoptera: Buprestidae: Buprestinae: Anthaxiini)
treatments.chunk_text: of the genus. Papers by Théry (1926) and Obenberger (1928a, 1928b) deal with the morphology of wings. Bionomy. According to Théry (1944) and according to the experiences of many collectors as well as my own field observation adult beetles are flower-visitors prefering yellow and orange flowers. Flowers of the genus Opuntia (Cactaceae) are very attractive although in the Paraguayan Chaco we found some specimens also on small, blue and pink flowers. During our collecting trip to Paraguay (2010) we used yellow, orange, violet and blue dishes as traps. The most effective were yellow and orange traps. The violet and blue traps were almost non attractive. Most of specimens of Tetragonoschema (Patagoschema subgen. nov.) cobosi sp.nov. were collected on flowers of Jarilla sp. (Caricaceae). Some species possess conspicuous, red and black elytral pattern imitating some species of Chrysomelidae (Clytrinae—Fig. 54) which could be some kind of mimetism or aposematic colouration (Hespenheide, 1975, 1996). FIGURES 19–27.

response.answer:
A great question about entomology!

Yes, Spain is home to a diverse range of colorful beetles. Here are a few examples:

1. **Scarabaeidae** (Dung Beetles): These beetles are known for their vibrant colors and patterns. Some species, like the **Glossina** genus, have metallic blue or green sheen on their elytra (wing covers).
2. **Lucanidae** (Stag Beetles): This family includes some of the most colorful beetles in Spain. The **Lucanus cervus**, also known as the "Horned Stag Beetle," has a striking red and black pattern.
3. **Cerambycidae** (Longhorn Beetles): Some species within this family, like the **Calosoma sycophagus**, have bright colors and patterns on their elytra.
4. **Carabidae** (Ground Beetles): While not always brightly colored, some ground beetle species in Spain, such as the **Carabus auronitens**, have striking yellow or orange markings.

Some specific examples of colorful beetles found in Spain include:

* The **Glossina fasciata**, a metallic blue-green dung beetle with black stripes.
* The **Lucanus cervus** (Horned Stag Beetle), which has a distinctive red and black pattern on its elytra.
* The **Calosoma sycophagus**, a longhorn beetle with bright yellow and black markings.

Keep in mind that the availability of certain species can vary depending on the region, season, and habitat. If you're interested in spotting colorful beetles in Spain, consider consulting with local entomologists or joining a guided insect-watching tour.
